title: Grounding review for #1367 compositional operation IR

references.label: Broader generated CLI runtime migration boundary

scope: operation-local fragments with generated Python and TypeScript execution, one representative report operation migrated, and docs/tests/checks updated
summary: The current operation model was flat operation -> ir_plan.steps -> primitive executor. The smallest implementation-ready composition slice is operation-local fragments executed by command-generation-owned Python and TypeScript runtimes, with one representative report operation migrated to the composed form.

review_method.operation_schema: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/operation.schema.json allowed ir_plan.steps but no reusable subflow or operation reference shape.
review_method.generator_wrapper: scripts/generate/workspace_command_generation.py harvested primitive usage only from top-level ir_plan.steps.
review_method.executors: command-generation rendered Python primitive_executor.py and TypeScript runtime.mjs with flat runSteps loops.
review_method.primitive_layout: AW still has large package runtime primitive files; #1357 now inventories exact runtime-boundary minimization routes while #1364 owns actual migration.

findings.finding: Report/lifecycle tails repeat select/load/emit or plan/apply/emit patterns. A local fragment model can express repeated subflows without introducing global hidden behavior.
findings.implementation_route: Add ir_plan.fragments plus uses_fragment call steps, then migrate config.report as a representative reporting pattern.

findings.finding: Generated Python and TypeScript targets are rendered from command-generation. Editing generated files or AW runtime primitives would violate the generated-code boundary.
findings.implementation_route: Implement fragment expansion in rickardvh/command-generation#7 and pin AW to that commit before refreshing generated targets.

findings.finding: Composition introduces missing-fragment, duplicate-fragment, ambiguous step, and cycle hazards.
findings.implementation_route: Executors reject those hazards; AW schema prevents ambiguous uses plus uses_fragment and fragment call arguments/outputs.

findings.finding: Splitting enormous runtime primitive files is downstream of the compositional model and #1364 migration inventory, not safe to complete in this first architecture slice.
findings.implementation_route: Document the primitive/fragment/operation/command ladder now; leave broad primitive extraction to #1364 after composition exists.

findings.finding: The new hierarchy supports #1366 by allowing tests at primitive, fragment, operation, and command levels instead of adding one-off command regressions for every composed subflow.
findings.implementation_route: Add command-generation primitive executor tests, AW contract/schema tests, generated freshness proof, and representative config command proof.

recommendation.scope: operation-local fragments with generated Python and TypeScript execution, one representative report operation migrated, and docs/tests/checks updated
recommendation.why_honest_for_1367: #1367 allows reusable fragments/subflows or operation references. This slice implements fragments/subflows with target parity and representative migration while explicitly leaving larger runtime migration work to #1364.

validation_commands: command-generation#7 tests for Python fragment execution and cycle rejection
AW operation schema and contract tooling validation
generated package refresh/check proving Python and TypeScript targets are current
focused config command tests proving composed config.report still behaves
